title: Chromatic Polynomial using Deletion-Contraction
description: Implementation of recursive deletion-contraction algorithm to compute chromatic polynomial
code:
def chromatic_polynomial(graph, edges):
 '''
 Compute chromatic polynomial using deletion-contraction
 graph: adjacency list representation
 edges: list of edges in graph
 Returns polynomial as dict of {power: coefficient}
 '''
 if not edges:
 n = len(graph)
 # Empty graph: k^n
 return {n: 1}
 
 # Pick first edge
 e = edges[0]
 remaining_edges = edges[1:]
 
 # DELTION: Remove edge e
 graph_del = [row[:] for row in graph]
 graph_del[e[0]][e[1]] = 0
 graph_del[e[1]][e[0]] = 0
 
 # Get new edges list for deleted graph
 edges_del = [edge for edge in remaining_edges 
 if edge != e and edge[::-1] != e]
 
 # CONTRACTION: Contract edge e
 # Merge vertex e[1] into e[0]
 graph_con = [row[:] for row in graph]
 for i in range(len(graph_con)):
 if graph_con[i][e[1]] == 1:
 graph_con[i][e[0]] = 1
 graph_con[e[0]][i] = 1
 graph_con[i][e[1]] = 0
 graph_con[e[1]][i] = 0
 
 # Get new edges for contracted graph
 edges_con = [(min(a,b), max(a,b)) for a,b in remaining_edges 
 if a != e[1] and b != e[1] and (min(a,b), max(a,b)) != e]
 
 # Recursive calls
 poly_del = chromatic_polynomial(graph_del, edges_del)
 poly_con = chromatic_polynomial(graph_con, edges_con)
 
 # P(G) = P(G-e) - P(G/e)
 result = {}
 for power, coeff in poly_del.items():
 result[power] = result.get(power, 0) + coeff
 for power, coeff in poly_con.items():
 result[power] = result.get(power, 0) - coeff
 
 return result
explanation: Uses recursive deletion-contraction to compute chromatic polynomial. For each edge, computes polynomial of graph with edge deleted and graph with edge contracted, then subtracts. Base case: empty graph gives k^n. Returns polynomial as dictionary mapping power to coefficient.

title: Chromatic Polynomial Evaluation
description: Evaluate chromatic polynomial at given k and find chromatic number
explanation: Helper functions to evaluate chromatic polynomial at specific k values and find the chromatic number by finding smallest k where polynomial value is positive. Also includes pretty-printing of polynomial.

title: Chromatic Polynomials for Common Graphs
description: Direct formulas for chromatic polynomials of common graph families
explanation: Direct implementations of chromatic polynomial formulas for common graph families: paths, cycles, complete graphs, trees, and empty graphs. These are O(n) or O(n^2) algorithms compared to exponential deletion-contraction.
